methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot is a Series E climate and energy technology company that has raised $206.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (Linazatech, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. Narrow peer comparables at the same stage were insufficient (zero exact matches, two adjacent-stage peers identified) to support a reliable median-multiple approach, necessitating reliance on stage-progression methodology.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $206.5M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, yielding $289M. This multiplier reflects typical valuation expansion from Series E to later-stage financing. The Peer-Median method was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient datapoints meeting same-stage and similar-tier filters within the broader Climate & Energy sector population of 21 companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $246M (low) to $332M (high), with a median estimate of $289M. Given reliance on a single applicable methodology, this range should be interpreted as reflecting stage-typical progression rather than cross-validated consensus. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.4x multiplier for StoreDot's specific risk profile, unit economics, and path to commercialization, as well as the limited precision afforded by a single-method approach. Additional private market comps at comparable stages and near-term financial performance would narrow confidence intervals materially.
